debateOfTheWeek.hook: Boil spice, peel quality, and price-per-pound drama — crawfish season is full-on argument mode.

seasonalRotation.note: When the heat hits, the flavor wars start.
First cold front hits and everyone swears their roux is darker.

items.description: Smoked, steamed, spicy, or classic — every parish swears theirs is the one true link.

contenders.caseFor: That snap, that spice, that consistency. This is the baseline everyone else gets judged against.
Rich without being greasy, balanced heat, and road-trip worthy every single time.
A little bolder and pepper-forward for folks who like their boudin with attitude.

items.description: The perfect cracklin is a religion here: loud crunch, rendered center, and just enough cayenne.

contenders.caseFor: Crispy outside, soft bite inside, and dependable seasoning from batch to batch.
The richness and pepper balance make this the bag people hide from their passengers.
Hardcore fans call it elite; purists argue texture. Either way, everybody has an opinion.

items.description: Boiled right means easy peel, deep seasoning, and zero mush. Anything less gets roasted in the group chat.

contenders.caseFor: Johnston Street loyalists ride hard for Dwight's spice profile and consistency.
Juicy, seasoned through, and consistently called out by Youngsville folks.
Rayne institution with heavy local trust and legit peel quality.

allSpots.note: Still a regular recommendation on Ridge.
Traditional boil style, no flashy gimmicks.

items.description: Dark roux debates get personal fast. Seafood vs chicken-and-sausage is practically a personality test.

contenders.caseFor: Deep roux flavor and seafood gumbo that keeps showing up in local best-of threads.
Generous crab and polished consistency if you want a classic bowl that never disappoints.
Beloved by traditionalists, debated by roux purists — which is exactly why it's in the conversation.

allSpots.note: Still gets serious love for depth and seafood balance.

items.description: Too thin and you're done. Too thick and they say it's gravy. Étouffée people are not forgiving.

contenders.caseFor: Classic crawfish base and texture that hits the sweet spot between silky and hearty.
Layered flavor and a richer body that étouffée fans keep chasing.
Underrated shout for folks who want bold crawfish flavor without overdoing the spice.

items.description: If the rice-to-gravy ratio is off, people will talk about it all week. Plate lunch standards are serious business.

contenders.caseFor: Big portions, deep seasoning, and that home-kitchen vibe that turns first-timers into regulars.
Dependable comfort lineup and one of the easiest yeses for lunch in Lafayette.
Not always first mentioned, but plate lovers know it quietly delivers.

items.description: Crust shatter, juicy meat, proper salt — this one starts arguments before the tray even hits the table.

contenders.caseFor: A sleeper hit with crunchy crust and legit seasoning that over-delivers.
Local icon status for a reason — the crust-to-juiciness balance is dialed in.
Beloved by many, debated by purists. The discourse never ends.

items.description: Chunky vs creamy, spicy vs mild — and everyone has one place they'd defend in court.

contenders.caseFor: Jalapeño chicken salad has a real cult following and sells out fast.
Multiple versions done right, including spicy options that keep regulars hooked.
A newer favorite for folks who want generous portions and strong flavor.

allSpots.note: St. Martinville pick with steady local praise.
Old-school favorite that keeps getting brought up.

items.description: Dressed or undressed? Fried shrimp or roast beef? This debate gets loud at every table.

contenders.caseFor: The benchmark. Big flavor, old-school bread, and lines out the door for a reason.
A legit local favorite with serious consistency and underrated range.
Beloved by loyalists, debated by bread purists — exactly the energy this list is about.

allSpots.note: Another strong contender depending on your order style.

items.description: Thick smash, thin smash, cheese edge, bun integrity — burger discourse is alive and unwell.

contenders.caseFor: Constantly brought up as a top-tier local burger when people start ranking favorites.
Hidden gem burger that surprises first-timers every time.
A no-nonsense burger stop with real neighborhood loyalty.

items.description: Thin crust, cheese pull, sauce sweetness — no one in Acadiana agrees and that's the fun of it.

contenders.caseFor: A longtime staple with deep local roots and undeniable nostalgia power.
Creative toppings and a signature style that has a real fan base.
Modern pie game with quality ingredients and lots of local chatter.

allSpots.note: Regularly mentioned by locals for old-school pizza nights.

items.description: Dry rub or sauce flood? Flats only or drum lovers? Wing takes get chaotic instantly.

contenders.caseFor: The most-cited local wing answer lately, especially if you like bold sauce options.
Turkey wings with serious flavor and plate-lunch-level soul.
Seafood spot that still throws down wings people keep talking about.

items.description: Street taco loyalty is fierce: tortilla quality, meat seasoning, salsa heat — all heavily judged.

contenders.caseFor: The pick locals keep repeating for no-frills, flavor-first street tacos.
Strong taco lineup with authentic flavor and great value.
Consistently praised when the conversation shifts to best authentic options.

allSpots.note: Known for specialty cuts and bold taco options.

items.description: Not just combo plates — this category is where authenticity debates and queso loyalty collide.

contenders.caseFor: Reliable crowd-pleaser with strong service and consistently solid plates.
The authenticity answer that keeps getting shouted out in local threads.
Loved by regulars, debated by taco purists — still firmly in the top-tier convo.

allSpots.note: University location still gets constant recommendations.

items.description: Roll purists vs specialty roll maximalists. Freshness, rice texture, and knife work are all on trial.

contenders.caseFor: Still the reference point locals compare others against when talking quality.
Downtown favorite for fish quality and consistency across the menu.
Atmosphere plus freshness keeps this in the serious sushi conversation.

allSpots.note: Family-owned and repeatedly recommended in Broussard.

items.description: Too sweet? Too strong? Perfect mix? Everyone has a favorite window and they'll defend it.

contenders.caseFor: A classic choice with broad flavor selection and reliable strength.
Flavor quality and consistency make this a standout if you're willing to drive.
Beloved for convenience and variety, debated on potency — peak daiquiri discourse.

allSpots.note: Keeps getting nods for flavor combos.

items.description: Texture matters. Syrup saturation matters. Condensed milk strategy definitely matters.

contenders.caseFor: The most repeated summer answer with strong texture and flavor consistency.
A warm-weather staple with loyal families and big flavor range.
Low-key but gets strong word-of-mouth every summer.

items.description: Smoke ring, bark, and sauce philosophy — BBQ arguments here can last longer than the cook itself.

contenders.caseFor: Boudin, smoked meats, and long-running local trust make this a heavy hitter.
A regular recommendation when people want classic, no-nonsense barbecue.
More people are treating this as a smoked-meat destination, not just a boudin stop.

items.description: Flaky shell, seasoned filling, and no dry bites. Meat pie lovers are very specific.

contenders.caseFor: The name still carries weight whenever true meat pie debates start.
A strong local option when you want a meat pie without leaving Acadiana.
Not the obvious answer, but locals keep shouting it out.

items.description: Fried shrimp quality, fish seasoning, and portion size. This is where value and flavor collide.

contenders.caseFor: Classic Acadiana seafood plate with consistency and quantity that satisfy.
A reliable platter pick with crispy fry and strong flavor.
Some call it the best all-around seafood plate, others prefer lighter fry.

items.description: Icing ratio, filling style, and baby-placement luck. Mardi Gras bakery loyalty runs deep.

contenders.caseFor: Classic done right: soft dough, balanced cinnamon, and no gimmicks needed.
Creative flavor twists that still respect what makes a king cake a king cake.
A dependable pick for people who like traditional style with bakery consistency.

items.description: Warm, saucy, and rich enough to share... except nobody actually wants to share.

contenders.caseFor: Big custard body and sauce that makes people scrape the bowl clean.
Rich but balanced sweetness — indulgent without crossing into overload.
An under-discussed dessert move that rewards the informed.

items.description: Pepper bite, chew texture, and smoke depth — glovebox snack rankings are not casual around here.

contenders.caseFor: Classic texture with enough spice to keep it interesting.
Bold seasoning and smoke-forward flavor for people who want more punch.
Less talked about, still a strong pick if you're already making a boudin run.

items.description: Tailgate hero food. If it bursts or dries out, your uncle will never let it go.

contenders.caseFor: Perfect grill snap and reliable seasoning profile.
A bolder flavor profile for folks who want more kick off the pit.
Strong supporters and equally strong opinions on seasoning style.
